title: Module port declaration without list of ports
body:
Hello,
Module declared without parenthesis are not supposed to allow port declaration (at least in Verilog2005 and SystemVerilog2017), but Icarus Verilog doesn't complain at all.
```
module a;
input x[2:0];
endmodule
```
(test taken from Yosys expected syntax errors [tests/errors/syntax_err05.v](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/master/tests/errors/syntax_err05.v), verilator also errors on this test)
In SystemVerilog 2017 this falls under the `module_ansi_header` case of the grammar and in Verilog2005 the second case of `module_declaration`.
Parsing it is fine but the user should at least be warned it is not standard compliant in order to avoid surprises.

Tested with the latest Arch Linux package iverilog (`iverilog -V` reports  `Icarus Verilog version 12.0 (stable) (v12_0-dirty)`)
